steps.title: Spot the tense pattern
steps.content: The entire passage is in present tense—”is a business,” “stands in contrast,” and “are shared” all use present forms. This establishes the timeframe for the sentence.

steps.title: Apply verb tense consistency
steps.content: When a sentence contains multiple clauses discussing the same timeframe, they must use consistent verb tenses. “Benefit” is the present form that matches the dependent clause and passage context. The other three options—past perfect, simple past, and past progressive—all violate this rule. That matches choice A — A is correct.
